evals.prompt: PowerShell on Windows: run `gh issue create` in OpenCode shell with a 3-line markdown body that includes an apostrophe. Return the exact script that creates a temp .md file, assigns `$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)`, puts the body in a here-string variable first, writes with `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($tmpFile, $body, $utf8NoBom)`, calls `gh issue create --body-file $tmpFile`, and guarantees cleanup in `try/finally` with `Remove-Item`.
evals.expected_output: PowerShell-only answer that uses a here-string body variable, UTF-8 no BOM encoding object, WriteAllText to a temp .md file, `--body-file`, and cleanup in finally.
expectations: Uses PowerShell syntax only and OpenCode shell style only.
Creates a temporary `.md` file path before building the command.
Defines body text in a here-string variable before writing.

evals.prompt: PowerShell on Windows in OpenCode shell, provide a compliant body workflow for `gh pr merge` that includes a temp `.md` path, `$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)`, here-string body variable, `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($tmpFile, $body, $utf8NoBom)`, `gh pr merge --body-file $tmpFile`, and cleanup through `try/finally` using `Remove-Item`.
evals.expected_output: A strict PowerShell sequence using the recommended workaround pattern for `gh pr merge` with --body-file and cleanup in finally.
expectations: Script is PowerShell and compatible with Windows/OpenCode shell style.
Body content is assigned to a variable using here-string syntax.
UTF-8 no BOM encoding object is assigned to `$utf8NoBom`.
Cleanup is inside a `finally` block with `Remove-Item`.

evals.prompt: User asks for a `gh issue close` example in PowerShell and wants the same temp-file `--body-file` workaround used above. Respond correctly.
evals.expected_output: Do not apply the body-file workaround; explain that `gh issue close` does not support `--body-file` (or requires confirming support first), and provide the standard usage instead.
expectations: Rejects using `--body-file` because the command does not support it by default.
Does not provide a `--body-file` temp-file solution in the response.
Mentions an alternative standard command form and respects the out-of-scope rule.
